- [21, 40] (c cell in left N container) - NO effect
- [21, 41] (c cell in left N container) - NO effect

Wait, let me look at the ascii more carefully:
```
Row 21: BgcNcccGcccNcgB
```
Col 38: N, col 39: c, col 40: c, col 41: c, col 42: G, col 43: c, col 44: c, col 45: c, col 46: N
So:
- [21, 39] toggles N bar ✓
- [21, 40] no effect
- [21, 41] no effect
- [21, 43] toggles N bar ✓
- [21, 44] unknown
- [21, 45] toggles N bar ✓

Looking at the positions:
- [21, 39]: right next to N at col 38
- [21, 40]: 2 cells right of N at col 38
- [21, 41]: 3 cells right of N at col 38 (next to G at col 42)
- [21, 43]: right next to G at col 42
- [21, 44]: 2 cells right of G at col 42
- [21, 45]: 3 cells right of G at col 42 (next to N at col 46)
So the c cells that toggle the bar are the ones ADJACENT to N or G cells:
- [21, 39]: adjacent to N at col 38
- [21, 41]: adjacent to G at col 42... but this didn't work!
- [21, 43]: adjacent to G at col 42
- [21, 45]: adjacent to N at col 46
Wait, [21, 41] is adjacent to G at col 42, but it didn't work. And [21, 43] is adjacent to G at col 42, and it did work. So adjacency to G doesn't explain it.
Let me think about this differently. Maybe the c cells that toggle the bar are the ones that are "inside" the N shape's opening. The N shape is:
```
NNN
N..
NNN
```
The opening is on the right side (cols 39-41 for left N, cols 43-45 for right N). The c cells inside the opening are:

- Right N: [21, 43], [21, 44], [21, 45]
But only [21, 39], [21, 43], and [21, 45] toggle the bar. [21, 40] and [21, 41] don't.
Hmm, [21, 39] is the leftmost c cell in the left container, [21, 43] is the leftmost c cell in the right container, and [21, 45] is the rightmost c cell in the right container.
Actually wait, let me reconsider. [21, 39] is adjacent to the N shape (col 38), [21, 43] is adjacent to the G divider (col 42), and [21, 45] is adjacent to the N shape (col 46).
So the pattern is: c cells adjacent to N or G shapes toggle the bar. But [21, 41] is also adjacent to the G divider (col 42), and it didn't work.
Wait, [21, 41] is at col 41, and the G divider is at col 42. So [21, 41] IS adjacent to the G divider. But it didn't work.
Hmm, let me re-examine. Maybe the segmentation groups c cells differently. Let me check the segmentation for the c cells.
Actually, I think the issue is that [21, 40] and [21, 41] are part of a different c cell object that's not "inside" the container. Let me check the segmentation.
Actually, I think the key insight is that [21, 39], [21, 43], and [21, 45] are all c cells that are part of the "container background" that toggles the bar, while [21, 40] and [21, 41] are c cells that are "inside" the N shape's opening and don't toggle the bar.

```
Row 20: BgcNNNcGcNNNcgB

Row 22: col 38-40 are NNN, col 41 is c, col 42 is G, col 43 is c, col 44-46 are NNN

- Right container: [20, 43], [21, 43-45], [22, 43] - these form a vertical strip at col 43 plus [21, 44-45]
Hmm, actually the c cells form a connected region inside each container. Let me think about which c cells toggle the bar:
- [21, 39]: toggles N bar (left container, adjacent to N shape)
- [21, 40]: no effect (left container, inside N shape opening)
